subject: Re: mdoc file issue for DM data
body:
Hi Alex,

Mdocspoofer won’t work for you, it’s written very specifically to deal with data coming from the thermo software ’Tomo’

You will have to generate some spoofed mdoc files yourself for your tilt-series - an example can be found at https://www.ebi.ac.uk/empiar/EMPIAR-10164/ <https://www.ebi.ac.uk/empiar/EMPIAR-10164/>

Good luck!

Alister


> On 16 Feb 2022, at 13:52, 'Alex Hempelmann' via Warp <warp-em@googlegroups.com> wrote:
> 
> Dear All,
> I am Alex and fairly new to the cryoEM world (coming from the x-ray world).
> I am trying to apply the EMPIAR tutorial steps to my data. Unfortunately, we have not collected the data with SerialEM, but DigitalMicrograph, thus we lack the mdoc.file.
> For the first step using .mdoc  (stack generation) it should be fine to just use newstack and get away with it.
> Later, after tilt-series alignment, the re-implementation into Warp by ''import tilt series from IMOD'' requires a .mdoc file yet again.
> 
> Anyone has a solution how I could bypass this step without .mdoc file? :)
> 
> I found in another thread  a nice tool called ''mdocspoofer'' for creating a dummy .mdoc file from tomo5 data. Would this work with DM data as well?
> 
> I tried to install it according to the instructions, but faced some difficulties:I attached the error log in a txt file here.
> 
> Maybe something obvious is off and someone could guide me into the right direction. Many thanks for any input on my issues!
> Best,
> Alex
> 
> -- 
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups "Warp" group.

> <mdocspoofer error output.rtf>
